candidates.why: Separate operating-level D&A line above operating profit, but it is not labelled as COGS/cost of sales and cannot be confidently attributed to the selected COGS bucket ('Materials and services') rather than SG&A/other functions.
Component of total D&A, but not labelled as COGS-related; using it would be more granular than the visible summary row and risks misallocation/double counting.
Component of total D&A, but not labelled as COGS-related; summary row exists and bucket attribution is unclear.

variables.notes: A separate D&A line exists at the same operating level as the selected buckets, so D&A does not appear embedded within 'Materials and services'. However, the excerpt provides only a company-wide D&A row and no COGS-specific D&A label, so adding it to COGS would risk misallocation or double counting.

candidates.why: Separate operating-level D&A line above operating profit and outside the selected XSGA rows ('Employee benefits' and 'Other operating expenses'), but it is a consolidated D&A line with no explicit SG&A-only attribution.
Component of total D&A, but no SG&A-specific wording; summary row should be preferred if any D&A were to be added.
Component of total D&A, but no SG&A-specific wording; summary row should be preferred if any D&A were to be added.

variables.notes: The selected XSGA bucket explicitly excluded D&A, and a separate D&A line is visible above operating profit. But the statement does not indicate how much of the total D&A belongs to SG&A versus COGS or other functions. Because only an unsplit summary row is available, conservative treatment is to avoid assigning it to XSGA automatically.
